idea.hook: How long would it REALLY take to reach the nearest star?
facts: Proxima Centauri, our closest stellar neighbor, is 4.24 light-years away from Earth.
Our fastest outgoing spacecraft, Voyager 1, travels at an incredible ~61,000 km/h.
At this speed, reaching Proxima Centauri would take over 75,000 years.
idea.payoff: Even our fastest human-made tech makes interstellar travel a multi-generational odyssey. The stars are truly distant!
idea.title: 🚀🌌 Reaching the Stars: How LONG Would It REALLY Take?!

scenes.text: How long to reach the nearest star?
scenes.visual: Dark space background with numerous faint stars, a subtle distant star glowing in the center.
Earth on the left, a small red dwarf star (Proxima Centauri) on the right. A dotted line or arrow appears between them, subtly indicating distance.

scenes.text: Our fastest craft: Voyager 1 at ~61,000 km/h.
scenes.visual: A stylized silhouette of Voyager 1 appears from the left, zooming across the screen to the right, leaving a subtle speed trail against the starfield.

scenes.text: At this speed, it takes >75,000 years!
scenes.visual: A numerical countdown/timeline graphic rapidly advancing, stopping dramatically at '75,000+' years. Stars twinkle faster in the background.

scenes.text: Interstellar travel: A multi-generational odyssey. The stars are truly distant!
scenes.visual: A vast, sweeping zoom-out from a tiny Earth, revealing immense emptiness and countless stars, emphasizing the scale of the cosmos. Voyager 1 fades into the distance.

script.thumbnail_text: How long to reach the stars?
